svg.title: Clock drift problem and how preamble fixes it
svg.desc: Two sections: top shows receiver sampling at wrong point due to drift, bottom shows preamble transitions letting receiver realign its sample point to the middle of each bit.
svg.text: Problem: receiver samples at the wrong moment
drift accumulates over time — sample point slides right
Fix: preamble gives receiver a transition every single bit
every transition = receiver measures exact bit boundary
sets its sample clock to fire halfway between transitions
What both sides are doing during preamble
sending 10101010... — nothing meaningful, just a steady rhythm
measuring time between each edge, adjusting its internal clock
receiver clock is locked — now it can reliably sample real data
